steps.title: Identify the structure around the blank
steps.content: The sentence reads "Lake Huron... is home ___ the largest island of them all: Manitoulin Island." The preposition "to" is followed directly by its object, the noun phrase "the largest island of them all."

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content: A preposition links to its object with no punctuation in between — you would never write "is home to, the island" or "is home to; the island." The blank should be just "to" with no extra mark.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'to;': a semicolon must join two independent clauses, but what follows is a noun phrase, not a clause. ✗
  • Choice B — 'to—': a dash would set off a parenthetical or appositive break, but no break belongs between a preposition and its object. ✗
  • Choice C — 'to': leaves the preposition attached to its object with no intervening punctuation, which is the standard form. ✓
  • Choice D — 'to,': a comma cannot separate a preposition from the noun phrase it governs. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
